int maxSatisfied(vector<int>& customers, vector<int>& grumpy, int minutes) {

    int n = customers.size();

    int base = 0;

    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {

        if (grumpy[i] == 0) {

            base += customers[i];

        }

    }

    // Now compute the max extra from the technique.

    int max_extra = 0;

    int current_extra = 0;

    for (int i = 0; i < minutes; ++i) {

        if (grumpy[i] == 1) {

            current_extra += customers[i];

        }

    }

    max_extra = current_extra;

    for (int i = minutes; i < n; ++i) {

        // remove the element leaving the window (i - minutes)

        if (grumpy[i - minutes] == 1) {

            current_extra -= customers[i - minutes];

        }

        // add the new element (i)

        if (grumpy[i] == 1) {

            current_extra += customers[i];

        }

        max_extra = max(max_extra, current_extra);

    }

    return base + max_extra;

}

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int maxSatisfied(vector<int>& customers, vector<int>& grumpy, int minutes) {
        int n = customers.size();
        int base = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            if (grumpy[i] == 0) {
                base += customers[i];
            }
        }
        int max_extra = 0;
        int current_extra = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < minutes; ++i) {
            if (grumpy[i] == 1) {
                current_extra += customers[i];
            }
        }
        max_extra = current_extra;
        for (int i = minutes; i < n; ++i) {
            if (grumpy[i - minutes] == 1) {
                current_extra -= customers[i - minutes];
            }
            if (grumpy[i] == 1) {
                current_extra += customers[i];
            }
            max_extra = max(max_extra, current_extra);
        }
        return base + max_extra;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution calculates the base satisfaction from non-grumpy minutes and uses a sliding window to find the optimal interval to apply the secret technique. The sliding window efficiently computes the maximum additional satisfaction possible by considering only grumpy-minute customers. This approach ensures linear time and constant space complexity.
